text:
Once upon a starry night, the wise old owl had a grand tour of the forest's most magical creatures. There was the clever fox who could turn into anything. There was the graceful eagle who could fly higher than any other bird.

One day, the owl thought to himself, "I wonder who could rival me?" Then he heard a tiny voice.

"Your kind is unique," the chipmunk said boldly.

"That's true," the owl admitted. "What about you?"

The chipmunk laughed softly.

"You're quiet...sometimes," the owl chided.

That night, the owl had a favorite owl book about wise owls. He wondered deeply about himself.

On a particularly stormy night, he fell deep asleep under the oak tree. He dreamed he became a wise owl himself.

Suddenly he saw a tiny chipmunk, just like him.

"I've learned so much," the wise owl whispered.

"You have." The chipmunk puffed his cheeks.
